metadata.title: Evaluation Bottleneck (B2)
metadata.description: As models get more capable, they get harder to evaluate. Benchmark gaming, contamination, and reward hacking compound. The system can't reliably tell whether the new model is actually better.
metadata.author: Tracking AI Course

nodes.label: Model Capability
nodes.description: Aggregate quality of the frontier model.

nodes.label: Model Complexity
nodes.description: Internal complexity that makes the system harder to evaluate against ground truth.

nodes.label: Evaluation Difficulty
nodes.description: How hard it is to know whether a new model is actually better, beyond benchmark gaming.

nodes.label: Reliable Progress
nodes.description: Rate at which actually-better models can be identified, validated, and shipped.

edges.description: More capable models are also more complex internally.
Hard evaluation introduces noise and slows verified improvement.
Slower reliable progress slows capability gains in the long run.

loops.label: B2: Evaluation Bottleneck
loops.description: The loop most underrated by people excited about autonomous research — you cannot self-improve faster than you can self-evaluate.
loops.behavior_pattern: Diminishing returns from capability gains as evaluation noise grows.
